pattern: Splitting the date on its dashes and converting each numeric part to binary independently reassembles a valid binary-dashed date with no leading zeroes in any segment.
hints: Split the date string on its two dashes to recover the year, month, and day as separate numbers.
Convert each of the three numbers to its binary representation independently; a language's built-in integer-to-binary conversion already omits leading zeroes.
Join the three binary strings back together with dashes in the same year-month-day order.

edge_inputs.description: a leap day near the top of the supported year range
single-digit month and day both need no padding removed from a length of one
a month value that is a power of two collapses to a short binary string
a year, month, and day that are all identical numerically

stress.description: random valid calendar dates within the supported year range
stress.note: always produces a real calendar date, matching the problem's guarantee
